qa_pairs.question: How do you open an Ansys Workbench project file in the graphical user interface?
qa_pairs.answer: To open an Ansys Workbench project file, which typically ends in .wbpj, first launch the Workbench GUI (as described in the "Graphical use" section of the document). Once the GUI is running, go to "File" and then select "Open" to choose and load your project file.

qa_pairs.question: What is the procedure to access the Extension Manager within Ansys Workbench?
qa_pairs.answer: To access the Extension Manager in Ansys Workbench, after opening your project file in the GUI, click on the "ACT Start Page." This action will open the "ACT Home page" tab. From there, select "Manage Extensions" to launch the Extension Manager.

qa_pairs.question: What are the steps to install and load an extension, such as AdditiveWizard.wbex, in Ansys Workbench?
qa_pairs.answer: To install an extension in Ansys Workbench, once you're in the Extension Manager, click the box with the large plus sign located under the search bar, then navigate to find and install your .wbex file (e.g., AdditiveWizard.wbex). To load the extension, you can either click to highlight its box for the current session only, or click the lower-right corner arrow in the extension's box and select "Load extension" to enable it for both current and future sessions.
